title: eurybia pygmaea
intro: Eurybia pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Eurybia, is a type of flowering plant in the aster family (Asteraceae), characterized by its small, daisy-like flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Eurybia pygmaea include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support immune function. Eurybia pygmaea is often prepared in herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to utilize its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
